name: Las Minas de Talco – circular between Lucar and Somontin (PR-A 301)
name_original: Las Minas de Talco – circular entre Lúcar y Somontín (PR-A 301)
region_intro: Hiking around Lúcar offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de las Estancias, a mountain range characterized by limestone formations and natural springs. The region features the prominent Piedra Lobera Natural Monument, a 1722m peak with dramatic scenery. Trails explore varied landscapes, including remnants of old talc mines and panoramic viewpoints overlooking the Almanzora Valley. This area provides a range of hiking experiences across different difficulty levels. Best hiking trails around Lúcar The most popular hiking route is Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail, an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a significant elevation gain of over 1500 feet (460 meters). Another top favourite among local hikers is Saw by the Era del Violín – circular (SL-A 13), a moderate 3.9 miles (6.3 km) path. This circular trail offers a shorter option for exploring the local landscape, with an elevation gain of over 770 feet (230 meters). Local hikers also love the Las Minas de Talco – circular between Lucar and Somontin (PR-A 301), an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through areas with historical talc mines, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. Hiking around Lúcar is defined by the Sierra de las Estancias mountain range, limestone formations, and natural springs. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy village loops to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Lúcar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Lúcar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Lúcar?
A: Lúcar is nestled in the Sierra de las Estancias, offering diverse terrain characterized by limestone formations and natural springs. You'll find trails exploring varied landscapes, from the dramatic scenery around the 1722m peak of Piedra Lobera Natural Monument to areas with historical talc mines. The region provides a mix of mountain ascents and more gentle paths.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or a relaxed walk?
A: Yes, Lúcar offers several easy trails. There are 3 easy routes available, including village loops that are great for any fitness level with easily accessible paths. These shorter options allow you to explore the local landscape without significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Lúcar?
A: Yes, Lúcar features several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983297317/saw-by-the-era-del-violin-circular-sl-a-13" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saw by the Era del Violín – circular (SL-A 13)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.3 km path. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984231133/las-minas-de-talco-circular-between-lucar-and-somontin-pr-a-301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Minas de Talco – circular between Lucar and Somontin (PR-A 301)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.4 km and takes you through areas with historical talc mines.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in Lúcar?
A: The Lúcar area is rich in natural attractions. You can explore the striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4625289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Piedra Lobera&lt;/a&gt;, a significant geological landmark. The region is also known for its numerous water features, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2547534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Perica Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2650515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Huevo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, you might encounter impressive gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2093686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cantaril Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Estrecho de Urrácal&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views of the region?
A: Yes, the municipality of Lúcar is home to several viewpoints that provide breathtaking panoramic vistas. These include La Santa, Lúcar, and Pastor, which offer stunning views of the Almanzora Valley and the Sierra de los Filabres, perfect for appreciating the serenity and splendor of the landscape.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Lúcar?
A: The routes in Lúcar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 16 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the natural beauty of the Sierra de las Estancias, and the variety of trails suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through historical sites or points of interest?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984231133/las-minas-de-talco-circular-between-lucar-and-somontin-pr-a-301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Minas de Talco – circular between Lucar and Somontin (PR-A 301)&lt;/a&gt; trail leads through areas with remnants of old talc mines, offering a glimpse into the region's industrial past.

Q: What is a moderately challenging hike I could try in Lúcar?
A: For a moderately challenging hike, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984324837/las-arrodeas-el-pinatar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 13.8 km long, takes approximately 4 hours and 12 minutes to complete, and features a significant elevation gain of over 460 meters, offering a good workout with rewarding views.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Lúcar area?
A: There are 14 hiking routes available around Lúcar. These include 3 easy trails, 9 moderate trails, and 2 more difficult options, catering to a wide range of hiking abilities and preferences.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the gorges around Lúcar?
A: Yes, the area features several impressive gorges. While not a specific route in this guide, you can explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2093686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cantaril Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Estrecho de Urrácal&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2649812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cerrada de Valentín (La Cerrá), Tíjola&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular points of interest in the wider region.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Lúcar?
A: Moderate hikes in Lúcar can vary in duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984324837/las-arrodeas-el-pinatar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 4 hours and 12 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984231133/las-minas-de-talco-circular-between-lucar-and-somontin-pr-a-301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Minas de Talco – circular between Lucar and Somontin (PR-A 301)&lt;/a&gt; is usually completed in about 3 hours and 51 minutes.

Q: Can I find trails with significant elevation changes for a more challenging experience?
A: Yes, Lúcar offers trails with significant elevation changes. Some routes require good fitness, taking up to 5 hours with up to 3,000 ft (900m) of elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984324837/las-arrodeas-el-pinatar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of over 460 meters, providing a substantial climb.
tips: Lúcar is a Spanish town and municipality located in the northwestern part of the Valle del Almanzora region, in the province of Almería. It borders with the Almeria municipalities of Tíjola, Armuña de Almanzora, Purchena, Somontín and Oria, and with the Granada municipality of Cúllar. The municipality is located at an altitude of 912 meters. The name Lúcar comes from the Latin “lucus”, which means jungle1. 🌿🏞️

Somontín is located on a natural terrace known as Balcón de la Almanzora, in reference to the Sierra on which it is located. Although there are no records of the origins of the town, its name of Latin origin indicates that it predates the Arab era.
